message:
fix: harden crypto RNG precision, error printing, async sleep, session
store directory creation, and file line counting

Multiple robustness improvements across core modules:

- crypto_shim.rs: Upgrade randomInt hostcall from 32-bit to 53-bit
  randomness (matching JavaScript's safe integer precision). Previously
  used 4 bytes → u32 → f64 which only gave ~4 billion distinct values;
  now uses 8 bytes → u64 >> 11 → f64 for the full 2^53 range that JS
  Number can represent. Also convert getrandom::fill panic to a
  tracing::error log to avoid crashing the entire CLI if the OS entropy
  pool is temporarily unavailable.

- main.rs: Switch error printing from Display ({err}) to Debug ({err:?})
  format to include the full anyhow error chain with context, making
  crash diagnostics much more actionable. Also replace blocking
  thread::sleep in the manual-input polling loop with async
  asupersync::time::sleep to avoid blocking the tokio runtime.

- session_store_v2.rs: Ensure the tmp/ parent directory exists before
  writing the index rollback tempfile, preventing ENOENT on fresh
  session stores. Also simplify fill_buf EOF detection to use the more
  idiomatic is_ok_and(<[u8]>::is_empty).

- tools.rs: Fix off-by-one in get_file_lines_async where files ending
  with a newline would produce a spurious empty trailing line. Now pops
  the phantom line when the normalized content ends with '\n', matching
  the behavior users expect from line-based file reading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
